raw_consumer_statement:
Complaint Summary I am filing this complaint against American Express National Bank for inaccurate credit reporting, failure to properly investigate disputed and fraudulent charges, improper billing of annual membership fees prior to card receipt or activation, and continued reporting of false delinquencies despite active disputes and good-faith payments.

American Express has reported my account as 30 and 60 days past due to consumer reporting agencies using incorrect balances, even though the amounts reported include disputed, unauthorized, and improperly billed charges that remain unresolved after more than a year.

Detailed Explanation Unauthorized and Improper Fees American Express charged annual/new member fees before I received or activated the card, without consent. This constitutes improper billing, misleading practices, and violation of consumer protection standards.

Timely Fraud and Billing Disputes I timely disputed fraudulent and incorrect charges. American Express acknowledged the disputes and stated the matters were under investigation but failed to complete a lawful investigation, failed to provisionally credit the account, and failed to correct the balance.

Good-Faith Payments Ignored Despite incorrect balances, I continued making minimum payments in good faith while awaiting proper adjustments and credits. American Express nonetheless assessed late fees and reported delinquencies.

False Credit Reporting American Express reported 30-day and 60-day late payments with inflated and inaccurate balances to XXXX and other bureaus XXXX despite knowing the account was disputed. This reporting has materially harmed my credit profile. 

Failure to Correct After Notice Even after multiple complaints and direct disputes, American Express failed to correct or delete inaccurate information, in violation of federal law.
